parsed_output.visible_action: Sella leans closer to the shared clinic display, pinches the routing trace wider with two fingers, and marks three telemetry gaps in amber before setting one palm flat against the bed-status column beside it.
parsed_output.spoken_text: The math supports review, not intake. I can hold a conditional slot until the next heat-debt update, and only if your rescue ledger names who carries repair, escort, and quarantine burden after dockfall. I will not turn a corrupted packet into a body by wanting it hard enough. I also will not let the tribunal make sanctuary spend the courage it declined to own.
parsed_output.private_interpretation: Maer is not grandstanding. That makes it worse, naturally, because honest rescue still eats air, beds, repair hours, staff hands, and the last clean patience in the room. The routing logic has the ugly shape of distress, but ugly shapes are not people, and Sella has buried enough almost-certainties to distrust any mercy that arrives before capacity accounting.
parsed_output.intended_effect: Keep the possible survivor inside the decision path without treating the packet as proof, force Maer to attach material obligations to the rescue claim, and prevent sanctuary from becoming the quiet place where everyone else deposits moral debt.

relationship_update_candidates.value: Sella acknowledges Maer's rescue ethic while requiring him to bind rescue language to logistics, ledger responsibility, and post-dockfall burden.

unresolved_hooks: Whether the next heat-debt update makes rescue physically survivable.
Whether Maer can name a credible repair, escort, and quarantine chain instead of handing the cost to sanctuary.
Whether the corrupted packet contains testimony, medical telemetry, firmware noise, or some unresolved mixture.

notes: The output uses scoped lore concepts such as heat-debt update, rescue ledger, dockfall burden, and sanctuary as moral-debt sink.
The response keeps packet personhood unresolved and frames Maer motive through Sella interpretation.
Candidate updates were returned as structured objects; this is accepted after widening the responder-output schema.
